title: Terraform Lambda and API Gateway module
description: Terraform modules for all 6 Lambda functions and the API Gateway REST API, mirroring template.yaml. IAM roles with least-privilege DynamoDB permissions. Environment variables wired from SSM Parameter Store.

acceptance_criteria.then: all 6 functions exist: get_current_metrics, ingest_metrics, get_sprint_list, get_sprint_detail, get_trends, get_story_detail
each role grants only the DynamoDB actions it needs — no Action=[*] or Resource=[*]
all 6 routes match the SAM template exactly: GET /metrics/current, POST /metrics/ingest, GET /metrics/sprints, GET /metrics/sprint/{num}, GET /metrics/trends, GET /metrics/story/{id}
DYNAMODB_TABLE and AWS_REGION are set from SSM Parameter Store references

acceptance_criteria.when: running checkov on infra/modules/lambda/ and infra/modules/api-gateway/

non_functional_requirements.security: Lambda execution roles use least privilege. No inline policies with wildcard actions. API Gateway has throttling configured.
non_functional_requirements.performance: Lambda memory set to 256MB for all functions. Timeout set to 30 seconds.

definition_of_done: All 6 Lambda functions defined in module
API Gateway routes match template.yaml exactly
agent_instructions: Read template.yaml before writing the module — it is the source of truth for Lambda handlers, routes, and IAM permissions. Mirror it exactly. Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. Do not use Action=[*] or Resource=[*] in any IAM policy.
